records.ety: [F. rfuter, L. refuteare to repel, refute. Cf. Confute, Refuse to deny.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Refuted; p. pr. & vb. n. Refuting.]

defs: To disprove and overthrow by argument, evidence, or countervailing proof; to prove to be false or erroneous; to confute; as, to refute arguments; to refute testimony; to refute opinions or theories; to refute a disputant.
quotes: There were so many witnesses in these two miracles that it is impossible to refute such multitudes.
